metadata.notes: agent=yes (/clear mints randomUUID → new transcript file); daemon=full (same-PID cleanup #169 emits transcript_removed); driver=gap:reset_session (stubbed arm)

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:reset_session

## Reasoning

**agent=yes.** Gemini CLI's `/clear` (alt name `/new`) is a built-in REPL command whose action mints a fresh session id and re-keys the transcript. From the v0.46.0 bundle (`packages/cli/src/ui/commands/clearCommand.ts`): `clearCommand = { name: "clear", altNames: ["new"], description: "Clear the screen and start a new session", action: async (context) => { … const newSessionId = randomUUID2(); config.resetNewSessionState(newSessionId); … await geminiClient.resetChat(); … } }`. `resetNewSessionState(sessionId)` calls `setSessionId(sessionId)`; `resetChat()` does `this.chat = await this.startChat()`, which constructs `new ChatRecordingService(context)`. On initialize the recording service computes `conversationFile = ${SESSION_FILE_PREFIX}${timestamp}-${this.sessionId.slice(0,8)}.jsonl` from the (now-rotated) session id — so `/clear` materializes a NEW `session-<ts>-<id8>.jsonl` under the same `~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/` dir and the old file stops being appended. This is functionally identical to claudecode's `/clear` UUID rotation.

**daemon=full.** The two scenario observables both have a concrete daemon mechanism:

1. *New session appears in ready with a NEW session_id.* The gemini-cli adapter's Source is `FilesUnderRoot{ Dir: ~/.gemini/tmp }` (agent.go), whose fswatcher recurses into the per-project `chats/` dirs (adapter.go). The new `*.jsonl` is detected; its first non-skip line (the opening user prompt; bootstrap `<session_context>` carries the workspace cwd) seeds a fresh session that lands in `ready`. cwd is harvested from the new file's `<session_context>` (parser.go `workspaceRe`), so PID discovery (pid.go `DiscoverPIDByCWDAndCmdLine`) binds the new session to the SAME launcher PID — the in-REPL reset keeps the process.

2. *Old session disappears.* `/clear` is one process, two session ids over time → both sessions hold the same launcher PID. When the new session binds that PID, `PIDManager.HandlePIDAssigned` collects every other non-subagent holder of that PID as stale and, per the #169 same-PID cleanup, emits `KindTranscriptRemoved` for the old session and broadcasts `PushTypeDeleted` + `repo.Delete(old)` (pid_manager.go L431-457). This path is keyed purely on PID — it is agent-agnostic and already the load-bearing mechanism for claudecode/codex/opencode/pi reset cells, so it carries straight to gemini-cli.

**driver=gap:reset_session.** The agent CLI and daemon can both do their part, but the gemini-cli interactive driver cannot yet ELICIT the reset. `replaydata/agents/gemini-cli/driver-interactive.sh` ships the `reset_session` arm STUBBED (`reset_session) not_implemented reset_session || break`) and `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"` deliberately omits it, so recipe-lint flags any recipe needing it as a semantic_gap before recording. `record` must port the seam (type `/clear`, then auto-detect the new `session-*-<id8>.jsonl` by scanning the project's chats/ dir, mirroring the resolve_transcript primitive already present) before it drives. This is tooling work, not an observability limit.

## Emission caveat (why confidence is 0.8, not 0.9)

The new-file creation chain is read straight from the bundle source (high confidence). The one property I cannot confirm purely from code is whether, after `/clear`, the OLD file goes fully quiet or the same process keeps touching it with `{"$set":{"lastUpdated":…}}` heartbeats. Even if it does, the parser skips `$set` heartbeats (no state transition) and the same-PID cleanup deletes the old session the moment the new session binds the PID — so the old-session-disappears assertion does not depend on the old file going silent. A live recording promotes this from provisional to settled; the spec asserts the correct behavior regardless.
caveats: Emission of the OLD file after /clear is not confirmable from code: if the same process keeps writing {"$set":{"lastUpdated"}} heartbeats to the rotated-out file, the parser skips them (no state transition) and the same-PID #169 cleanup still deletes the old session when the new one binds the PID — so the spec's old_session_ended is independent of it. Live recording settles this.
/new is documented as an exact altName of /clear (same action, same UUID rotation) — the recipe covers /clear only; /new would re-record identically.

The new post-/clear session binds the launcher PID via cwd harvested from the new file's <session_context>; this is the same EnrichNewSession m.LastCWD fallback the initial column onboarding live-verified, so binding on a fresh same-cwd session is expected to hold.

sources.ref: ~/.nvm/.../@google/gemini-cli/bundle (clearCommand.ts, v0.46.0): name "clear", altNames ["new"], action mints randomUUID2() → resetNewSessionState(newSessionId) → setSessionId → geminiClient.resetChat()
sources.note: Agent-side proof that /clear rotates the session id.
Proof the rotated session id produces a NEW transcript file under chats/ — the signal the daemon's FilesUnderRoot watcher detects.
PID-keyed (agent-agnostic) emit of KindTranscriptRemoved + PushTypeDeleted for the old session — the 'old session disappears' observable.
FilesUnderRoot ~/.gemini/tmp recursing into chats/; new-file birth → ready; cwd harvested from <session_context> binds the new session to the launcher PID.
Driver gap: the reset_session step type is stubbed and must be ported before record.

preconditions: gemini CLI on PATH and authenticated (Google OAuth oauth-personal)
setup: Driver launches `gemini -y` under tmux; gemini mints its own session id (harvested from the first transcript's header). The reset_session step types /clear, then auto-detects the SECOND session-*-<id8>.jsonl created in the project chats/ dir and re-points subsequent turns at it.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: fresh

verify: events.jsonl shows the FIRST gemini session entering ready, then a working/ready cycle for turn 1
after /clear, events.jsonl emits transcript_removed (and/or process_exited) for the FIRST session id — the 'old session disappears' assertion
events.jsonl contains a DIFFERENT session id appearing in ready after /clear (new session_id, fresh row)
the second turn's working/ready cycle is on the NEW session id, not the old one
